Patient.title: Patient
Patient.description: Represents a patient using the GlycoAI app.

height.description: Height of the patient in centimeters.

weight.description: Weight of the patient in kilograms.

age.description: Age of the patient in years.

insulinSelections.description: Up to two insulin selections, including brand and type.

id.description: Unique identifier for the insulin dosage event.

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the insulin was administered.

GlucosePrediction.title: GlucosePrediction
GlucosePrediction.description: Represents a blood glucose prediction generated by the AI model.

id.description: Unique identifier for the glucose prediction.

predictionTime.description: Timestamp for which the blood glucose level is predicted.

reasoning.description: Reasoning behind the prediction from the AI inference tool.

AiTip.title: AiTip
AiTip.description: Represents an AI-generated tip or suggestion for managing blood glucose.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AI tip.

tipText.description: The actual text of the tip or suggestion.

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the tip was generated.

GlucoseBehaviorExplanation.title: GlucoseBehaviorExplanation
GlucoseBehaviorExplanation.description: Represents an explanation for unusual blood glucose behaviors.

behaviorDescription.description: Description of the unusual blood glucose behavior.

explanation.description: Explanation of why the behavior is occurring, including reasons.

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the explanation was generated.

IssueSolution.title: IssueSolution
IssueSolution.description: Represents a solution for common blood glucose issues.

issueType.description: Type of blood glucose issue (e.g., dawn phenomenon, nighttime hypoglycemia).

solutionText.description: Text describing the solution to the issue.

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the solution was generated.

definition.description: Stores patient data.  Owned by the user with ID '{userId}'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who owns the patient data.

definition.description: Stores insulin dosage events for a specific patient, owned by the user with ID '{userId}'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who owns the patient data.

params.name: insulinDosageId
params.description: The ID of the insulin dosage event.

definition.description: Stores glucose prediction data for a specific patient, owned by the user with ID '{userId}'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who owns the patient data.

params.name: glucosePredictionId
params.description: The ID of the glucose prediction.

definition.description: Stores AI tips for a specific patient, owned by the user with ID '{userId}'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who owns the patient data.

params.name: aiTipId
params.description: The ID of the AI tip.

definition.description: Stores glucose behavior explanations for a specific patient, owned by the user with ID '{userId}'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who owns the patient data.

params.name: glucoseBehaviorExplanationId
params.description: The ID of the glucose behavior explanation.

definition.description: Stores issue solutions for a specific patient, owned by the user with ID '{userId}'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who owns the patient data.

params.name: issueSolutionId
params.description: The ID of the issue solution.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore structure is designed to provide a secure and scalable solution for the GlycoAI app, focusing on authorization independence and clear data segregation. The core idea revolves around storing all user-specific data under the `/users/{userId}` path to establish clear ownership and simplify security rules. This eliminates the need for complex `get()` calls in security rules.  Each entity associated with a user (InsulinDosage, GlucosePrediction, AiTip, GlucoseBehaviorExplanation, IssueSolution) will be stored in subcollections under the respective patient document to further enhance data segregation and enforce ownership. Since `Patient` contains personally identifiable data, it's stored directly under `/users/{userId}`. The design uses hierarchical paths (`/users/{userId}/patients/{patientId}/...`) to represent the 1:N relationships, providing a clear and secure ownership model. This design prioritizes Authorization Independence (CRITICAL). By structuring the data with explicit ownership, the security rules can be written to allow a user full access to their own data, and nothing else.  This approach supports secure `list` operations (QAPs) since listing data under `/users/{userId}/patients/{patientId}/...` inherently scopes the query to the current user's data. The absence of global roles simplifies the overall security model.
